content: Sturges: You know, Preston, I always wondered what the world was like before the bombs dropped. Preston Garvey: The world was a very different place, Sturges. People lived in relative peace and security. We had technology that allowed us to communicate instantly across the globe, and we were beginning to understand the nature of the universe itself. Sturges: I can only imagine what it must have been like. And the women... what were they like before? Preston Garvey: Women were... different, I suppose. They were respected and valued for their intellect and their abilities, not just for their bodies. They weren't subjected to the same objectification and degradation that they are now. Sturges: Yeah, I guess that's true. But hey, at least we can still find comfort in each other's company, right? Preston Garvey: Indeed, Sturges.

Preston Garvey: Thanks for your company, Sturges. I could use someone to talk to in this dark world we live in. Sturges: Anytime, Preston. We all need a friend sometimes, especially in these trying times. Oh, by the way, I found a Perception Bobblehead over there. Might come in handy for us to keep our eyes peeled for trouble. Preston Garvey: Great! I can always use the extra perks. You never know what dangers lurk in the dark.
